User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the ChatServe Bot application, identified by their phone number and a unique Firebase UID.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity (Firebase UID).

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number, used for Firebase Phone Authentication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a single SMM service order placed by a user through the ChatServe Bot.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

platform.description: The social media platform for which the service is ordered (e.g., 'Instagram', 'YouTube').

link.description: The URL for the post, profile, or video related to the order.

quantity.description: The requested quantity for the SMM service (e.g., number of followers, likes, views).

price.description: The total price for the ordered service.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order status was last updated, typically by an admin.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a chat message exchanged between a user and the bot within the WhatsApp-style UI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity within a user's chat.

userId.description: Reference to the User who sent or received this message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

sender.description: Identifies the sender of the message ('user' or 'bot').

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Defines the available Social Media Marketing services offered by the bot for various platforms.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

platform.description: The social media platform this service applies to (e.g., 'Instagram', 'YouTube').

name.description: The descriptive name of the SMM service (e.g., 'Followers', 'Likes', 'Views', 'Subscribers').

basePricePerUnit.description: The base price for one unit of the service, used for calculating total order price.

unitName.description: The unit of measure for the service (e.g., 'follower', 'like', 'view', 'subscriber').

description.description: A brief description explaining what the service offers.

definition.description: Stores user profile data, identified by their Firebase UID. This is private data, accessible only by the user themselves. The userId in the path provides primary authorization.
Stores chat messages exchanged between a user and the bot. Each message is owned by the user whose userId is in the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this message.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific message.

definition.description: Stores SMM service orders placed by a user. Each order is associated with the user via the path, allowing users to create and read their own orders. Admin users can access all orders via collection group queries. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed this order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific order.

definition.description: Stores the definition of all available SMM services offered by the bot. This data is static and publicly readable by all users to browse available services. Admin users have write access.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific SMM service.

definition.description: An 'existence-based' collection to grant administrative privileges. If a document exists at this path for a given userId, that user is an administrator. Used for DBAC (Database Access Control) without custom claims.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is granted administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence by denormalizing authorization context into document paths and applying Structural Segregation to ensure homogeneous security postures within collections. This design facilitates simple, robust, and easily debuggable security rules while adhering to QAP (Queries are not Filters) principles.

1.  **Users (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level `users` collection. This path-based ownership (`{userId}` in the path matching `request.auth.uid`) makes user profile data inherently private, allowing for straightforward rules (e.g., `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`).
2.  **User-Specific Messages (`/users/{userId}/messages/{messageId}`):** Messages are stored as a subcollection under the specific user's document. The `userId` in the path ensures that users can only access their own messages. This directly supports QAPs, as `list` operations on `/users/{request.auth.uid}/messages` will only return the current user's messages without needing a `where` clause in the rules. The `Message` document will contain a `userId` field (denormalized) to maintain consistency with the entity schema and provide an explicit reference, though the path itself dictates ownership.
3.  **User-Specific Orders (`/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`):** Similar to messages, orders are also stored in a user-specific subcollection. This enables users to create and read their own orders via path-based authorization (`request.auth.uid == userId`). For administrators, a 'Collection Group Query' on the `orders` subcollection allows them to retrieve all orders across all users, while security rules for the collection group (`match /{path=**}/orders/{orderId}`) can grant admin-specific read/write access. The `Order` document will include a `userId` field (denormalized) for explicit ownership reference.
4.  **Public Services (`/services/{serviceId}`):** All available SMM services are stored in a top-level `services` collection. This data is public and read-only for all users, supporting QAPs for listing available services without any filters. Administrators will have write access to manage this list.
5.  **Admin Roles (`/roles_admin/{userId}`):** Administrative privileges are managed using a dedicated top-level `roles_admin` collection. The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}` indicates admin status (DBAC - Database Access Control), allowing for simple and explicit checks in security rules (e.g., `allow read, write: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid));`). This avoids `get()` calls on arbitrary paths, enhancing Authorization Independence.

This structure ensures that user-owned data is inherently segregated and authorized by path, critical for `list` operations (QAPs). Global, public data is easily accessible, and administrative access is clearly defined through an existence-based role collection, all without relying on complex hierarchical data dependencies in security rules or custom claims.
